description.p: Works with password stores that use the standard pass layout, so you can keep using the password folders you already have.

ul.li: Open, search, and edit one or more password stores
Make passwords and copy passwords, usernames, and one-time login codes
Edit entries with easy fields or as plain text
Check for weak passwords and see repeated details like usernames, emails, and web addresses
Add an existing store, make a new one, or restore one from Git
Import passwords from other apps on supported Linux setups
Choose which keys unlock a store, including password-protected keys, FIDO security keys, and OpenPGP smartcards
Create and import keys
Sync Git-backed stores, manage Git remotes, and sign Git commits
For extra-sensitive stores, require more than one key to open them
Add a selector for nested .gpg-id files on the recipients page, so folder-specific recipients can be managed.
Keep recipient changes scoped to the selected folder-specific .gpg-id file instead of always rewriting only the root recipients file.
Prefer a usable local private key when multiple recipients are configured, instead of picking an unrelated coworker recipient first.
Harden search privacy so the GNOME search provider exposes less metadata and no longer matches against secret fields.
Reduce the risk of secrets leaking through failed commands and logs by redacting sensitive command output more aggressively.
Improve secret handling with safer private file writes, stronger process hardening, and tighter recipient validation.
Add a primary Save and go back action in the pass file editor.
Add an extra add-item and clear search row at the bottom of the pass file list.
Hide apply buttons until required input is filled in across the pass file editor, new-item popup, key dialogs and other related forms.
Improve keyboard navigation with better arrow-key support and smoother focus movement across the app.
Removed the FIDO2 backed password stores feature from the flatpak app. Contact me if you want it back.
Added FIDO2-protected OpenPGP keys.
Fixed OTP bug where valid secrets were incorrectly invalidated.
Added FIDO2 guides and progress indicators.
Change the default to use the filename as the username.
Only enable FIDO2 features if the integrated backend is used.
Add documentation
Add search profider
You can now open pass files in a new window
Add OpenPGP hardware key support for private keys
Expand the password editor with cleanup actions
You can now use the field values as variables in search
Add the option to copy the logs
Fix optional Git synchronization
Changed the app icon to a keycord
Show disabled options instead of hiding them
Add tools page
Add advanced search
Add optional GPG synchronization
You can now manage Git remotes

description.p: You can now generate new private keys.
